description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray surface, accented by patches of rusty orange and tan hues. The stone has a rough, natural texture with visible striations and color gradients, suggesting mineral deposits or weathering effects. One side features a broad, flat face with a prominent rusty streak, while the opposite side is flatter and marked with handwritten text. The edges are uneven and angular, with two sides appearing much thinner than the others, giving the stone a wedge-like profile. The surface finish is matte and unpolished, with visible micro-texture and some minor chips or abrasions. This stone would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: An irregular, gray stone with rusty orange streaks and a rough, matte surface, featuring both broad and thin angular faces.
description_view_0: The stone's broadest face is visible, showing a mix of gray and tan with a prominent rusty orange streak running diagonally.
description_view_1: A thin, edge-on view reveals the stone's wedge-like profile and highlights its angular, uneven thickness.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is mostly gray with some orange mottling and visible handwritten text, appearing flatter than the front.
description_view_3: Another thin edge view, this time showing a lighter, more tan-colored surface with less visible texture detail.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with rusty orange streaks and patches, no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with minor chips and abrasions, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; not covered by vegetation.
